event: Interior of the St. Antoniuskapel in the St. Janskerk at Utrecht
symbols: soaring vaulted ceiling — aspiration toward the divine, lifting the soul upward
white-washed walls — Protestant austerity and the primacy of the Word over images
light streaming through windows — divine illumination entering the sacred space
small human figures dwarfed by architecture — the humility of humanity before God's greatness
description: A meticulously rendered interior of a Dutch Gothic church. The view looks down a side aisle with tall white-washed walls and a ribbed vaulted ceiling in golden-brown tones. Large arched openings on the right lead to adjacent aisles. The floor is a geometric pattern of blue and white tiles. Small figures sit and stand in the middle distance, dwarfed by the massive architecture. Memorial plaques and a decorative piece hang on the walls. Light enters from unseen windows, creating subtle gradations of brightness on the white surfaces.

_meta.title: The St. Antoniuskapel in the St. Janskerk at Utrecht
